name: yogurt bread
instructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9x5 loaf pan with parchment and spray with cooking spray.

In a large bowl, whisk together 3 cups all-purpose flour, 1 1/2 tablespoons baking powder, and 3/4 teaspoon salt. Add 1 3/4 cups + 2 tablespoons of 2% plain yogurt (NOT Greek). Mix with a spatula until a dough forms.

Transfer the dough to the loaf pan and smooth the top. Bake at 350°F for 60 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

Let the loaf cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack and cool for another 25 minutes before slicing. Use a serrated knife; cuts into 10 slices.
notes:
External recipe from iFoodReal (Olena Osipov). 6g protein per slice; yields 10 slices.

NO GREEK YOGURT: this recipe is calibrated for regular plain yogurt, which is wetter than greek. Greek yogurt is too thick and bakes differently — the dough will come out wrong. If you only have greek, find a different recipe (the source site has a separate greek yogurt bread).

YOGURT FAT %: default measurements above are for 2% yogurt. For 3.5% (whole milk) yogurt, use 2 cups + 2 tablespoons of yogurt with the same 3 cups of flour. Bake time stays the same.

FLOUR SUBSTITUTION (using AP + baking powder + salt instead of self-rising): the original recipe calls for 3 cups self-rising flour. We use 3 cups all-purpose flour + 1 1/2 tablespoons baking powder + 3/4 teaspoon salt as a direct substitute. This is what's encoded in the instructions above. If you ever have self-rising flour on hand, just use 3 cups of it and skip the baking powder and salt.

MEASURING FLOUR: spoon flour into the measuring cup and level with a knife. Scooping directly from the bag compacts the flour and throws off the ratio.

VARIATIONS (suggested by the original):
- Herb and garlic: 1 tsp dried rosemary or thyme + a pinch of garlic powder folded in.
- Cheese: up to 1/3 cup shredded cheddar, parmesan, or another firm cheese folded in.
- Seeds and nuts: sunflower seeds, chia seeds, or chopped nuts in the dough.
- Dried fruit: 1/3 cup raisins, cranberries, apricots, blueberries, dates, or figs.
- Topping: sprinkle sesame seeds or everything-bagel seasoning on top before baking.
